description_long: This object is a manually operated electric drill featuring a metallic body with two prominent wooden handles for grip and control. The central section houses a set of visible gears, likely for torque transmission, and a long cylindrical shaft extends from the main body, ending in a drill chuck with a pointed bit. The rear of the drill is fitted with a black, rounded support pad, possibly for bracing against a surface or the user's body. The metallic surfaces are smooth and slightly reflective, while the wooden handles have a matte finish. The overall construction suggests durability and ergonomic handling, suitable for heavy-duty drilling tasks in urban maintenance or construction settings.
description: A metallic hand-crank electric drill with two wooden handles, visible gears, and a pointed drill bit, designed for manual operation and durability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two wooden handles extending outward and the black support pad at the rear.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the drill, the gear mechanism, and the alignment of the handles.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the black support pad and the symmetrical placement of the handles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the drill bit, gear assembly, and the ergonomic handle arrangement.
